page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-180 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 180 dedicated vCPUs derived from 90 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The system is equipped with 720 GB of RAM, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with the instance placing in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, Geekbench multi-core scores, and Passmark CPU evaluations. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, achieving top-tier status in both cached small-block reads at 13454.22 GB/sec and uncached large-block reads at 356.6 GB/sec. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16, which contribute to top-tier performance in cryptography, database operations, and static web serving throughput. Single-core performance is strong, ranking in the top 25% across Geekbench and stress-ng single-core benchmarks.

While the instance delivers top-tier performance in multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, and LLM inference prompt processing, its single-threaded gzip compression and small-model text generation speeds sit in the average tier. The lack of bundled local storage requires external block storage attachment, which represents a key architectural tradeoff. Given its resource density and high multi-core efficiency, the instance is qualitatively cost-effective for highly parallelized workloads. It is highly suited for large-scale database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-180 is a general-purpose server instance featuring 180 dedicated vCPUs and 720 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor on the x86_64 architecture. With 90 physical cores and multithreading, it delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and exceptional memory bandwidth, reaching 356.6 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. The hardware supports AVX-512 instructions, enhancing cryptographic and database operations. However, it lacks local storage and GPUs, requiring external storage solutions. This instance is highly optimized for parallel processing, making it an excellent fit for large-scale database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-180 is a general-purpose server featuring 180 vCPUs, 720 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC processor, delivering top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-180 features 180 vCPUs, 720 GB RAM, and AMD EPYC processors for high-performance multi-core workloads.
tagline: A high-density, multi-core x86_64 platform delivering exceptional memory bandwidth and compute performance for demanding parallel workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 180-vCPU AMD EPYC 9B14 processor with dedicated core allocation
Equipped with 720 GB of RAM, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth up to 356.6 GB/sec
Supports AVX-512 and cryptographic instruction sets for accelerated processing
No local storage or GPU resources included, requiring external storage attachment
Best suited for large-scale databases, software compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference
